interpretationCaveats: AV-block-label semantics: only the LONG pause-beat is labeled 'AVB'. The following short compensatory beat is 'LS' (long-short pair). This matches the physiological event — the AV-block IS the pause; the next beat is just the rhythm returning to baseline.
Vagal AV-block in trained horses is NORMAL — not pathological.
Equine HRV reference ranges are species-specific — DO NOT apply human Kubios baselines.
Single-session metrics need ≥5min stable resting epoch to be reliable.
Drug effects (beta-blockers, sedatives) dominate HRV signal — see subject.currentMedications.
Recent shoeing suppresses Stress Index for 120min (Pechanova 2024).
observations: RMSSD 100.2ms is ABOVE typical healthy range (>80 ms; Stucke 2015). Common in well-trained horses with strong vagal dominance — confirm via DFA-α₁ and Poincaré pattern.
Baevsky Stress Index (normalized √SI) = 2.76. relaxed / parasympathetic dominant.
⚠ Cross-validation flag: LF/HF differs 82% between Welch (PSD) and Lomb-Scargle methods. Cite LF/HF with explicit method qualifier; do not over-interpret the absolute value in this session.
recommendedNextSteps: All metrics within healthy reference ranges. Continue routine monitoring.

applicabilityChecks.citation: Stucke D, et al. (2015). Diurnal variation of HRV in horses. Equine Vet J 47(2):184-189.
applicabilityChecks.rationale: All cohort criteria met — bands directly applicable

applicabilityChecks.citation: von Borell E, et al. (2007). HRV as a measure of autonomic regulation of cardiac activity for assessing stress and welfare in farm animals — a review. Physiol Behav 92(3):293-316.
Mitchell K J, et al. (2018). Atrial fibrillation in horses: clinical and diagnostic features. Equine Vet J 50(2):189-194.
applicabilityChecks.rationale: RMSSD=100ms below 200ms cutoff OR HR=34bpm in bradycardic range — Mitchell 2018 hint not triggered for this session

applicabilityChecks.citation: Vitale V, et al. (2020). HRV in horses with naturally-occurring gastrointestinal disorders. J Vet Intern Med 34(3):1284-1291.
applicabilityChecks.rationale: Vitale 2020 cohort = horses presenting with acute abdominal symptoms. Asymptomatic 'rest' session does not match study cohort — colic-indicator cutoffs are not validated for screening healthy horses.

applicabilityChecks.citation: Plews D J, Laursen P B, Stanley J, Kilding A E, Buchheit M (2013). Training adaptation and HRV in elite endurance athletes: opening the door to effective monitoring. Sports Med 43(9):773-781.
applicabilityChecks.rationale: Dynamic store has 0 sessions (<7) AND no real lnSD recorded for subject — Plews framework runs on literature-default lnSD=0.15 (population estimate). z-scores are interpretable but use a generic SD, not a person-specific one. Treat as 'pre-firm-baseline' assessment.

applicabilityChecks.citation: Task Force of the European Society of Cardiology and the North American Society of Pacing and Electrophysiology (1996). HRV: standards of measurement, physiological interpretation, and clinical use. Circulation 93(5):1043-1065.
applicabilityChecks.rationale: Session ≥5min minimum — Task Force 1996 standard met for time-domain stability. Note: Task Force 1996 is human-derived; equine extrapolation is standard practice but cite Stucke 2015 / von Borell 2007 for equine-specific bands.

30.label: LS

75.label: LS

76.label: LS

92.label: X

93.interpretation: Supraventricular ectopic beat — premature atrial contraction (PAC) suspect. Sporadic = benign; clustered → atrial pathology workup.
93.label: S

94.label: LS

107.label: S

108.interpretation: Supraventricular ectopic beat — premature atrial contraction (PAC) suspect. Sporadic = benign; clustered → atrial pathology workup.
108.label: S

109.label: LS

248.label: LS

249.label: LS

250.label: LS

251.interpretation: Long-short compensatory beat following a pause — rhythmic recovery, not a separate arrhythmia event.
251.label: LS

254.label: LS

255.label: LS

rmssd_low.source: Vitale 2020 — colic-specific (NOT Stucke 2015 generic stress band <20)

acuteColic.disclaimer: Akut-Indikator, KEINE Diagnose. Vitale 2020 + Gehlen 2020 wurden an Pferden mit klinischen Symptomen bei Klinikaufnahme validiert. Tierärztliche Untersuchung ist Pflicht.
acuteColic.recommendation: Bei klinischen Symptomen (Wälzen, Bauchschmerzen, Apathie) sofort Tierarzt kontaktieren. Indikator basiert auf HRV-Mustern symptomatischer Kolik-Pferde — keine Frühwarnung bei asymptomatischen Tieren.

afibHint.cutoffReconciliation: 302 ms = Mitchell 2018 confirmed-AFib cutoff (high specificity). referenceRanges.afibSuspectRMSSDms.low = 150 ms is the generic Poincaré-cluster investigation threshold (high sensitivity, covers vagal AV-block bursts as well as early AFib). Both thresholds are correct for their respective screening tiers.
afibHint.disclaimer: AFib bei trainierten Pferden mit hohem Vagaltonus möglich auch ohne klinische Bedeutung. Cutoff 302 ms basiert auf Mitchell 2018 — Pattern-Erkennung, KEINE Diagnose.
afibHint.recommendation: Standard-EKG durch Tierarzt zur AFib-Differenzierung empfohlen. AFib ist die häufigste leistungslimitierende Arrhythmie beim Pferd und gut behandelbar bei früher Diagnose.

asthmaScreen.disclaimer: Screening-Indikator basiert auf Random-Forest-Modell (Accuracy 90% in 20+20 Pferden, MANOVA Wilks' λ=0.18 p<0.001). Keine Diagnose. Differenzierung sEA / mEA / IAD nur durch Tierarzt möglich.
asthmaScreen.recommendation: Tierärztliche Abklärung mit BALF (Bronchoalveoläre Lavage) als diagnostischer Goldstandard empfohlen. Klinische Symptome zusätzlich beachten: Husten, Atemnot, Leistungsabfall.

clinicalIndicators.universalDisclaimer: CARLON Equine indicators are RESEARCH SCREENING TOOLS based on 64 peer-reviewed primary studies — NOT diagnostic devices. All triggered alerts require veterinary consultation. HRV patterns can suggest but never confirm specific diseases. See schema.preprocessing.references for full source list.

crossValidation.differentiator: Method-comparison metadata (Welch vs Lomb-Scargle, RMSSD vs SD1×√2, SampEn vs MSE-scale-1) ships in every CARLON export. Not present in Kubios Premium / Polar Flow / Garmin Connect / WHOOP / Withings / etc.

alternative.method: Lomb-Scargle periodogram on unequal-sampled RR (no interpolation)

lfHfRatio.interpretation: Lomb-Scargle avoids spline-interpolation bias. >15% divergence flags spectral analysis instability per Clifford 2002.

crossValidation.note: Self-verification: each main metric computed via two independent methods. Agreement <5% = methods cross-confirm; >15% = data instability flag. Standard in HRV methodology papers (Clifford 2002, Sassi 2015). DIFFERENTIATOR: This self-verification metadata is absent from Kubios Premium, Polar Flow, Garmin Connect, and every other commercial HRV export reviewed (2024-2026) — CARLON-Equine ships it in every session.

rmssdMs.interpretation: Methods agree to <1% — analytical identity confirmed.

alternative.referenceCitation: Costa, Goldberger & Peng 2002, Phys Rev Lett 89:068102

sampleEntropy.interpretation: Two independent implementations of the same Richman-Moorman algorithm. Discrepancy >5% flags numerical-stability issue (template-matching tolerance edge cases).

ecg_event_snippets_availability.clinical_consequence: AV-Block differentiation (vagal vs. Mobitz I vs. Mobitz II) cannot be confirmed from THIS export alone. For waveform-based differentiation use the Live-EKG export, or pair with a clinical ECG strip during a vet visit.
ecg_event_snippets_availability.how_to_obtain_snippets: Open the horse profile → Live-EKG → Start recording → Stop → Use 'Full Recording Export'. The resulting JSON includes µV waveform plus 10s snippets around each clinical event.
ecg_event_snippets_availability.reason: This export is from an RR-only recording ([on-device]-Offline or HR-aggregated). The Polar H10 µV ECG waveform is only available during a Live-EKG-Stream session — that produces a separate 'carlon_equine_ecg_full_recording_v1_8_1' JSON which contains ecg_event_snippets.

integrity.canonicalForm: rrMs values from rrData[].rrMs, formatted '%.2f', comma-joined (no spaces)
integrity.verify: python3 -c "import json,hashlib; d=json.load(open('export.json')); rrs=[x['rrMs'] for x in d['rrData']]; s=','.join(f'{r:.2f}' for r in rrs); print(hashlib.sha256(s.encode()).hexdigest())"

explicitNonResults: Session is too short for reliable VLF — VLF values reported but should not be interpreted clinically.
LF/HF cross-validation flagged 82% discrepancy between Welch and Lomb-Scargle — LF/HF ratio should be cited with explicit method qualifier.
limitations.note: This block explicitly states what THIS session CANNOT reliably measure. Standard in scientific publications, virtually absent in commercial HRV exports. Use perMetricFinalConfidence < 0.7 as 'cite with caution' threshold.

metricReliabilityDowngrades.dfaAlpha2: VERY_LOW — window 17-64 beats requires ≥256 beats (longer recording)
metricReliabilityDowngrades.lfPowerMs2 / lfHfRatio: LOW — session below 10-min minimum for stable LF estimation; cubic-spline interpolation may dominate
metricReliabilityDowngrades.vlfPowerMs2: VERY_LOW — <3 cycles within VLF-band, estimate statistically unreliable

labelToCountMapping.AVB: normalCount with avBlockCount-side-channel (vagal AV-block — physiologically normal in resting horses per Reef 1998)
labelToCountMapping.LS: shortBeatCount (long-short compensatory pair — the SHORT half is counted, marking the early-beat artifact)
labelToCountMapping.V: ectopicCount (ventricular ectopic / PVC) — NOT typical in equine

equineSpecific.baselineCountSourcesNote: baselineSampleCounts.dynamic_30d.totalSessionsInStore = full longitudinal store size. dynamic_30d.validPerMetric.<metric> = sessions with sufficient variance for THIS metric (may differ when some sessions were too short/noisy for stable per-metric stats). subject_profile_static / subject_profile_estimated_sd = static-profile baselines used when dynamic-store insufficient.

dynamic_30d.exclusionReasons: Sessions in store exceed per-metric valid count: some sessions had insufficient duration (<300s) or >25% artifact rate for stable per-metric variance estimate.

equineSpecific.equineQualityInterpretation: Good · echte Artefakte <5% nach AVB-Korrektur · Z-Score-Quellen: dynamisch 30-Tage (n=25)
equineSpecific.motionContextNote: ACC-Stream Gangart-Klassifikation — Phase-C (Polar H10 ACC + Walk/Trot/Canter-Heuristik)

baevsky.method: raw z-score (dynamic store, clamped ±5σ, min-SD floor 0.3)

meanHR.method: raw z-score (dynamic store, clamped ±5σ, min-SD floor 3bpm)

meanHRRecoveryDirection.method: raw z-score inverted for recovery semantics

rmssd.method: raw z-score (dynamic store, clamped ±5σ, min-SD floor 8ms)

sdnn.method: raw z-score (dynamic store, clamped ±5σ, min-SD floor 12ms)

frequencyDomain.methodology: Direct-DFT band-integration · Equine bands (Stucke 2015, von Borell 2007) · variable bin-step (VLF 0.001 Hz, LF 0.002 Hz, HF 0.005 Hz) · Goertzel-style power-at-frequency on 4-Hz cubic-spline-resampled signal · Welch-segmented CI (256-sample windows, 50% overlap, Hamming) · bypasses legacy vDSP_fft_zripD SIGSEGV

lombScargle.methodology: Lomb-Scargle periodogram on raw (uninterpolated) RR timestamps — no 4-Hz cubic-spline interpolation bias
lombScargle.reference: Clifford 2002 MIT PhD; Lomb 1976; Scargle 1982; equine: Gupta 2016 EMBC

mse.reference: Costa, Goldberger, Peng 2002 Phys Rev Lett 89:068102

nonLinear.cviLogBase: log10 (Toichi 1997 J Auton Nerv Syst 62:79–84 original)
nonLinear.dfaAlpha1ReliabilityNote: n=325 beats, bootstrap CI width=0.63 — cite with CI.

stationarityTest.reference: Dickey & Fuller 1979 JASA; MacKinnon 1996 J Appl Econometrics

symbolicDynamics.reference: Porta 2001 IEEE Trans Biomed Eng; Guzzetti 2005 Circulation

timeDomain.interpretation: RMSSD 100.2ms — above-typical, trained-horse vagal dominance (Stucke 2015) · SDNN 132.3ms — within healthy overall-variability band (von Borell 2007)

avBlockCount.note: Equine vagal AV-block is physiologically NORMAL in resting trained horses (Reef 1998 / von Borell 2007) — NOT pathology unless symptomatic. Differential vs Mobitz I/II requires ECG-strip.
avBlockCount.snomedCtLabel: Atrioventricular block (disorder) — equine-context: vagal AVB is normal in trained horses

dfaAlpha1.note: Detrended Fluctuation Analysis short-term scaling exponent (Peng 1995, window 4-16 beats). No LOINC code; cite Peng 1995 directly.

hfPowerMs2.loincLabel: Heart rate variability - high frequency power (HF)
hfPowerMs2.note: Equine HF band 0.07-0.6 Hz (Stucke 2015), differs from human 0.15-0.4 Hz.

lfHfRatio.note: Cite with explicit method qualifier (Welch vs Lomb-Scargle, see crossValidation.lfHfRatio).

lfPowerMs2.loincLabel: Heart rate variability - low frequency power (LF)
lfPowerMs2.note: Equine LF band 0.01-0.07 Hz (Stucke 2015), differs from human 0.04-0.15 Hz.

pNN50Pct.loincLabel: Heart rate variability - percentage of NN intervals differing >50ms (pNN50)

rmssdMs.loincLabel: Heart rate variability - root mean square of successive differences (RMSSD)
rmssdMs.note: LOINC 80394-6 is human-derived but widely used in equine HRV literature. Use FHIR R4 Observation with code.coding.system='http://loinc.org'.
rmssdMs.snomedCtLabel: Heart rate (observable entity) — HRV variability index

sampleEntropy.note: Sample Entropy (Richman-Moorman 2000) m=2, r=0.2·SDNN. No LOINC; cite Richman-Moorman directly.

sd1Ms.note: Poincaré-plot SD1 (Brennan 2001) — short-axis = √(½) × RMSSD identity. No LOINC code as of 2026-05; use vendor-specific extension.

sd2Ms.note: Poincaré-plot SD2 (Brennan 2001) — long-axis = √(2·SDNN² − ½·RMSSD²). No LOINC code as of 2026-05.

sdnnMs.loincLabel: Heart rate variability - standard deviation of NN intervals (SDNN)

privacy.note: Demo-Datensatz — Subjekt-Identität entfernt. Halter-/Tierarzt-Daten werden generell nie exportiert (Privacy-by-Design).

wasDerivedFrom.rawDataSource: Polar H10 · on-device offline recording @ 1024 Hz

reliabilityReference.note: Change > MDC95 is statistically detectable at p<0.05
reliabilityReference.source: van der Weide et al. 2021 (Utrecht University, equine 5-min rest)

reproducibility.floatingPointMode: IEEE-754-double (Swift Double on arm64/x86_64) — Accelerate vDSP uses same FP semantics. NO denormal-as-zero flag. Reproducibility verified across iPhone 12-17 + macOS Intel/ARM (same byte-output for same input).
reproducibility.note: Re-running the same rrIntervalsMs through this build of the app produces a byte-identical export, EXCEPT for: schema.generated timestamp, provenance.wasGeneratedBy.startedAt/endedAt, and aggregationOptIn.consentTimestamp (if changed). Strip those three fields before computing a top-level reproducibility-hash. The integrity.rrDataSha256 alone is deterministic given the input.
reproducibility.rngAlgorithm: SplitMix64 (Vigna 2015) — passes BigCrush statistical test suite
reproducibility.seedRationale: Fixed seed (42) for SplitMix64 bootstrap-RNG. Choice is arbitrary but pinned — across reruns of the same rrIntervalsMs, sd1CI95/sd2CI95/dfaAlpha1CI95/sampleEntropyCI95 are byte-identical. Changing the seed shifts CI bounds by ~1-3% (sampling noise within the bootstrap-distribution itself), not the central values.

aggregationOptIn.note: Owner has NOT opted in to research aggregation. Default-off. To opt in, set in Settings > Privacy > Research-Contribution and re-export.

researchContribution.note: Research-Contribution = export-ready metadata for Open-Science aggregation. Opt-in is owner-controlled (default false). Fingerprint allows deduplication across re-exports without revealing PII. Aggregation targets are aspirational — opt-in does not auto-submit; it marks the session as eligible for the owner to manually contribute via the contactURL.

openHRVPlatform.description: Cross-species HRV reference platform (human + equine + canine) for comparative autonomic-nervous-system research.
openHRVPlatform.eligibility: Any session with cross-validation block present (this schema v1.7.1+), method-comparison metadata required.

vitalkulturEquineHRV2026.description: Open-source equine HRV reference database aggregating anonymized rest-session metrics across breeds, ages, and fitness levels — addresses the gap that 90% of published equine HRV cutoffs come from <5 small studies (Stucke 2015, von Borell 2007, Vitale 2020).
vitalkulturEquineHRV2026.eligibility: Any rest-session ≥5 min, anonymization required (subject.id stripped, only fingerprint hash retained), motion-context = rest or mixed.

schema.jsonSchemaStatus: draft (local file in CarlonEquine/Schemas/, URL not yet hosted)
schema.name: carlon_equine_v1

preprocessing.artifactStrategy: Classified beats marked in rrData.label; non-finite values dropped before metrics; AV-blocks preserved (equine-physiological per Reef 1998)
preprocessing.detrending: Tarvainen 2002 Smoothness-Priors (Accelerate-vDSP) on resampled signal before FFT
preprocessing.resampling: Cubic-spline interpolation at 4 Hz uniform grid (Task Force 1996 standard)

standardsApplied: Peng et al. 1995 (Detrended Fluctuation Analysis α1)
von Borell 2007 (equine HRV in welfare assessment)

selfDocumentation.note: Three audience-tailored summaries of the SAME underlying data. Owner-summary: lay language, no jargon. Vet-summary: clinical band references + differential diagnoses. Researcher-summary: full quantitative detail + reproducibility pointers.

keyFindings: RMSSD (Entspannungs-Indikator): 100 ms — über typischem gesunden Bereich — bei trainierten Pferden meist positiv
nextActions: Diese Messung in dein Tagebuch aufnehmen
Bei wiederholt niedrigen RMSSD-Werten (<25 ms) Stress-Faktoren überprüfen
Vor wichtigen Entscheidungen immer Tierarzt konsultieren
owner.summary: Mistral hatte eine 9-minütige Messung mit 325 Herzschlägen. Der Vagus-Wert (RMSSD) liegt über typischem gesunden Bereich — bei trainierten Pferden meist positiv.

keyFindings: Equine reference: Stucke 2015 + von Borell 2007 + Vitale 2020 + Gehlen 2020. See referenceRanges + clinicalIndicators.
researcher.summary: Equine HRV session, polar-H10 RR @1024Hz, n=325, dur=567.5s. Lipponen-Tarvainen 2019 classifier; Tarvainen 2002 smoothness-priors detrending. Bootstrap-CI (Politis-Romano 1994). See provenance + crossValidation for full methodology.

keyFindings: Time-domain: RMSSD 100.2ms — OBERHALB Stucke 2015 healthy band (30-80) — vagal dominance oder AV-Block-Verdacht
SDNN 132.3ms — innerhalb von Borell 2007 healthy band (50-200)
Frequency-domain interpretation: see crossValidation.lfHfRatio für Welch-vs-Lomb-Diskrepanz-Check
nextActions: Holter-EKG bei klinischen Symptomen UND HRV-Auffälligkeit
vet.summary: 9-min HRV-Messung, n=325. RMSSD 100.2 ms, SDNN 132.3 ms, Mean-HR 34 bpm, AVB-Count 0. Quality Good — <5% artifacts, acceptable for research.

perturbations.interpretation: AVB exclusion changes Mean HR by <5% → robust to AVB events
perturbations.name: exclude_avb_beats

perturbations.interpretation: AVB+LS exclusion changes Mean HR by <5% → robust
perturbations.name: exclude_avb_and_ls

perturbations.description: Recompute on first half of session (n=162 beats)
perturbations.interpretation: First half within 15% of full-session value → stationary signal
perturbations.name: first_half

perturbations.description: Recompute on second half of session (n=163 beats)
perturbations.interpretation: Second half within 15% of full-session value → stationary signal
perturbations.name: second_half

meanHRBpm.rationale: All perturbations change Mean HR by <10% — result is robust to outlier-removal and epoch-choice.

perturbations.interpretation: AVB exclusion changes RMSSD by <5% → robust to AVB events
perturbations.name: exclude_avb_beats

perturbations.interpretation: AVB+LS exclusion changes RMSSD by <5% → robust
perturbations.name: exclude_avb_and_ls

perturbations.description: Recompute on first half of session (n=162 beats)
perturbations.interpretation: First half shifts 23.7% — non-stationary; consider epoch-windowing
perturbations.name: first_half

perturbations.description: Recompute on second half of session (n=163 beats)
perturbations.interpretation: Second half shifts -30.8% — non-stationary; consider epoch-windowing
perturbations.name: second_half

rmssdMs.rationale: Perturbation swings RMSSD by ≥30% — result is dominated by a small number of beats or by non-stationarity. Cite with explicit perturbation context.

perturbations.interpretation: AVB exclusion changes SDNN by <5% → robust to AVB events
perturbations.name: exclude_avb_beats

perturbations.interpretation: AVB+LS exclusion changes SDNN by <5% → robust
perturbations.name: exclude_avb_and_ls

perturbations.description: Recompute on first half of session (n=162 beats)
perturbations.interpretation: First half within 15% of full-session value → stationary signal
perturbations.name: first_half

perturbations.description: Recompute on second half of session (n=163 beats)
perturbations.interpretation: Second half within 15% of full-session value → stationary signal
perturbations.name: second_half

sdnnMs.rationale: At least one perturbation shifts SDNN by 10-30% — cite with awareness of which beats/epochs dominate.

sensitivityAnalysis.note: Per-metric re-computation under 4 perturbations: exclude AVB-beats, exclude AVB+LS, first half, second half. Robustness label aggregates max |deltaPct|: <10% = high (robust), 10-30% = medium (cite with context), ≥30% = low (result driven by few beats / non-stationarity).

wfdbMetadata.format: RR-interval series (equivalent to WFDB .ann annotation file)
